findings.description: mask_middle with visible=0 defeats masking: value[-0:] evaluates to value[0:] (the entire string), so the output becomes mask_chars followed by the FULL plaintext, completely echoing the secret it is meant to hide (CWE-201 information exposure).

Caller-controlled mask_char breaks the masking guarantee: mask_char="" makes both the full-mask branch (line 55) and the middle (line 56) empty, so mask_middle returns an empty string or concatenated first+last chars with no masking and no length indication, silently under-redacting secrets (CWE-201).
A multi-character mask_char inflates the output and makes its length a function of the original value length, leaking the exact length of the masked secret to anyone observing the output.

levenshtein has no input-length cap and runs in O(len(a)*len(b)) time / O(len(b)) space; if either argument is attacker-controlled at a public endpoint (e.g. fuzzy search / typo suggestions), large inputs cause CPU and memory exhaustion (algorithmic-complexity DoS, CWE-407/CWE-1333-class).

slugify applies NFKD normalization + ASCII fold + invalid-char stripping, so distinct Unicode inputs (homoglyphs, full-width, combining sequences) can collapse to identical slugs; if slugs are used as identifiers/keys this enables collision and resource/identity confusion (CWE-1007/CWE-178).

slugify and truncate are text formatters, not output encoders; routing their results into HTML/SQL/shell/path contexts without contextual escaping at the (out-of-scope) call sites is injection-prone — advisory note since no sink is in scope and the module is honestly documented as helpers.
